script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah. July 14th, 2026. It's already feeling pretty humid out there.
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。2026年7月14日です。もう外はかなり蒸し暑いですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Good morning, Alex. Yes, it is. Looks like it’s going to be overcast all day, with a high of 33 degrees.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。そうね。一日中曇り空で、最高気温は33度になりそうよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ugh, really? And I'm still trying to wake up. This is my second coffee already.
script.ja: うわ、まじですか？まだ全然目が覚めてないのに。もう2杯目のコーヒーですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Sounds about right. I'm on my first cup of tea.
script.ja: あなたらしいわね。私は今、お茶を一杯飲んだところよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Did you see that news about the hip-hop granny? The 'Rice Dance' video?
script.ja: ヒップホップおばあちゃんのニュース見ました？あの「ライスダンス」のビデオ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I did. She's quite impressive. Lots of energy.
script.ja: 見たわ。かなり印象的だったわね。すごいエネルギー。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Right? I was like, 'Wow, go granny!' It's kind of inspiring, actually.
script.ja: でしょ？「うわ、おばあちゃんすごい！」って思いました。結構感動しますよね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. And speaking of rice, they're still pushing rice flour for sweets, aren't they?
script.ja: そうね。米と言えば、まだ米粉をお菓子に使うことを推進しているのね？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, I saw that. To boost consumption. I feel like I read this every year, though. That's not going to change at my office, we still eat a ton of regular rice.
script.ja: ええ、見ました。消費を促進するためって。でも、毎年このニュースを読む気がしますよ。うちの会社じゃ変わらないだろうな、みんな普通のご飯を山ほど食べてますから。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: For home bakers, it offers another option.
script.ja: 家庭でパンやお菓子を作る人にとっては、別の選択肢になるわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Maybe. My kid won't care yet. He just eats whatever I put in front of him, usually. It's a lot of work, though. Even at one year old, he keeps us busy.
script.ja: そうかも。うちの子はまだ気にしないでしょうけどね。だいたい何でも目の前のものを食べますから。でも大変ですよ。1歳なのに、もうずっと忙しいです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They definitely do. Mine kept me on my toes until they were teenagers.
script.ja: 本当そうよ。うちの子たちも、ティーンエイジャーになるまで私をずっと忙しくさせてたわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, I also saw something about a clothing company making costumes for mobile game characters.
script.ja: あ、あと、モバイルゲームのキャラクターの衣装を作るアパレル会社のニュースも見ましたよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Cosplay is certainly a big business now. I imagine a lot of kids will be excited.
script.ja: コスプレは今や大きなビジネスになっているわね。たくさんの子供たちが喜ぶでしょうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: My kids will probably be into that kind of stuff eventually. They're already obsessed with screens, so...
script.ja: うちの子も、たぶん最終的にはああいうのにハマるんでしょうね。もうすでに画面に夢中だし。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's the world they're growing up in.
script.ja: それが彼らが育つ世界なのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah. Oh, hey, I just remembered, isn't today Famicom Day? Or tomorrow?
script.ja: うん。あ、そういえば、今日ってファミコンの日じゃないですか？それとも明日でしたっけ？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Tomorrow, actually. July 15th, 1983. That's when Nintendo released the Family Computer.
script.ja: 明日よ。1983年7月15日。任天堂がファミリーコンピュータを発売した日ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, right. I thought it was today. Man, that takes me back. I remember begging my parents for one of those *gadgets*.
script.ja: ああ、そうか。今日だと思ってました。いやー、懐かしいな。あの「ガジェット」が欲しくて両親にねだったのを覚えてますよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: A gadget is a small, often electronic, device that does something useful or new.
script.ja: ガジェットというのは、小さくて、たいてい電子的な、何か役立つことや新しいことをする装置のことよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Exactly! It was definitely new and fun. I spent so many hours on it. It's funny how quickly technology changes. That Famicom is like, *vintage* now.
script.ja: その通りです！まさに新しくて楽しかった。何時間もあれで遊んでましたよ。技術の変化って本当に早いですよね。あのファミコンなんて、もう「ヴィンテージ」ですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's definitely from an earlier time, yes. Something classic and of high quality, but from the past.
script.ja: 確かに昔のものね。クラシックで高品質なものでも、過去のものといった感じね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, classic. My kids would probably just look at the graphics and laugh today.
script.ja: ええ、クラシック。うちの子たちは多分、今日のグラフィックを見たら笑うだけでしょうね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Mine still play those old games sometimes, actually. They appreciate the history, I think.
script.ja: うちの子たちは、今でもたまに古いゲームをやるわよ。歴史を評価しているんだと思うけど。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, really? That's cool. I thought they'd only be into the latest stuff. My son is just obsessed with his phone.
script.ja: あ、そうなんですか？それはすごいな。最新のものしか興味ないと思ってました。うちの息子なんてスマホに夢中ですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Well, they grew up with them. It's different.
script.ja: まあ、彼らはスマホと一緒に育ったからね。違うのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I jump at everything these days. Pretty sure it's the baby.
script.ja: 最近は何にでも飛び上がっちゃいますよ。たぶん赤ちゃんのせいですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It happens. Just be glad he's mostly sleeping through the night now.
script.ja: そういうものよ。夜にほとんど寝てくれるようになっただけでもありがたいと思いなさい。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: He mostly does. Thank goodness. My wife and I were zombies for a while there.
script.ja: ほとんど寝てくれます。本当によかった。しばらくの間、妻と私はゾンビみたいでしたから。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I remember those days. I made a big batch of pasta sauce over the weekend. Helps a lot with weeknight dinners.
script.ja: あの頃を思い出すわ。週末にパスタソースを大量に作ったのよ。平日の夕食にすごく役立つわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That's smart. I should try that. We're always scrambling to figure out what to cook.
script.ja: それは賢いですね。僕もやってみよう。いつも何を作るか慌ててますから。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It saves a lot of time and effort.
script.ja: 時間も労力もかなり節約できるわよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Okay, I think I'm actually awake now. This Daily Tanu-chan Show is starting to feel lively.
script.ja: よし、これでやっと目が覚めました。この『デイリーたぬちゃんショー』も盛り上がってきた感じですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's getting there.
script.ja: そうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Alright, let's get this day going.
script.ja: よし、一日を始めましょうか。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Sounds good.
script.ja: ええ、そうね。

news.summary: Last year, we were blown away by a trio of grandmothers who showed off some impressive hip-hop moves as they danced to the title track from Bruno Mars’…

news.title: Clothing company to bring popular Japanese mobile games’ character costumes to life
news.summary: We can’t get enough of cosplay, so we welcome news that clothes company Nutte, in conjunction with mobile phone game makers Axel Mark, have obtained an official license…

wikipedia.title: ファミコンの日
wikipedia.description: 1983年、任天堂が家庭用テレビゲーム機「ファミリーコンピュータ」を発売した。
